discrepancy_analysis:
A. QUESTION
   ANOVA p-value for mean beta at Other (non-CpG-island) probes across 7 methylation subgroups (Fig 1d right, n=108).

B. REPORTED vs MODEL
   - Reported: p=1.8e-13 (ANOVA)
   - Model:    p=4.585e-13 (F=16.33, df=6,101); 629,933 non-Island probes; N=108.
   - Relative difference on raw p-values: |4.585e-13 - 1.8e-13| / 1.8e-13 = 1.55 (155%), which exceeds the ±10% threshold.
   - Scientific interpretation: both p-values are overwhelmingly significant (on the order of 1e-13); the scientific conclusion (strong methylation-subgroup separation at non-CpG-island probes) is identical.

C. INVESTIGATION
   The model reproduced the ANOVA with N=108 samples and a non-Island probe set of 629,933 probes, yielding F=16.33 on 6,101 degrees of freedom and p=4.585e-13. The paper reports p=1.8e-13. Both values are in the same extreme-tail regime (1e-13). At p-values this small:
     - Floating-point precision and the exact F-to-p conversion (e.g., scipy.stats.f.sf vs R pf) can shift the reported value by a factor of 2-3.
     - Minor differences in the non-Island probe set (e.g., probe filtering for SNPs, cross-reactive probes, sex chromosomes, or detection-p thresholds) change the per-sample mean beta slightly and thus the F-statistic.
     - Whether subgroup labels were taken from the final consensus clustering (post-reassignment) or an intermediate assignment can shift F marginally.
     - Annotation file version (IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19 vs ilm10b2/ilm10b5) alters which probes are annotated as 'Island' vs 'Other'.

D. MAGNITUDE
   MINOR in scientific terms (same order of magnitude, identical conclusion). However, the raw numeric ratio (~2.5x) is >10%, so the numeric-concordance test marks this DISCREPANT.

E. ROOT CAUSE
   Likely a combination of: (a) different EPIC annotation build for Island/Other classification, (b) slightly different probe-filtering QC (SNP/cross-reactive/sex-chromosome removal), and (c) inherent numerical instability of p-value reporting at ~1e-13. No indication the paper or model mis-specified the ANOVA design.

F. PROPOSED FIX
   Match the paper's probe-filtering pipeline exactly (same minfi preprocessing, same SNP/cross-reactive filter, same annotation build). Use the same R function (aov / anova.lm) as likely used in the paper rather than scipy.stats.f_oneway, which may differ in final-digit precision at extreme p-values. Both approaches will yield p < 1e-12; the exact digit is not scientifically meaningful.

G. CONFIDENCE
   MEDIUM. The scientific finding is robustly reproduced; the numeric mismatch is in a regime where p-value precision is dominated by implementation choices.
proposed_fix: Align probe filtering (SNP/cross-reactive/sex chromosome) and EPIC annotation build (ilm10b4.hg19) with the paper, and compute ANOVA with the same software (R aov) used in the paper. Both model and paper yield p<1e-12; the exact digit is not scientifically meaningful.
